Title: Verify successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions: System is running; no existing user with ID 1001
Test Steps:
1. Call `newId()` to generate a new user ID (assume returns 1001).
2. Create a new `Utente` object with provided attributes.
3. Call `setPassword("SecurePass123!")`.
4. Call `setEmail("john.doe@example.com")`.
5. Call `setNome("John")` and `setCognome("Doe")`.
6. Verify `getName()` returns "John" and `getCognome()` returns "Doe".
Expected Result:
- User object is created successfully with all attributes set.
- No errors during method calls.
- Retrieved name and surname match input.

Title: Verify password update with valid and invalid inputs
Preconditions: Existing user with ID 1001 and password "SecurePass123!"
Test Data:
- Valid new password: "NewSecurePass456!"
- Invalid new password: "weak" (too short), "123456" (no special char), "NoUppercase123!" (no uppercase)
Test Steps:
1. Call `setPassword("NewSecurePass456!")` on user with ID 1001.
2. Verify password is updated (assume system validates internally).
3. Attempt to call `setPassword("weak")` and log any errors.
4. Attempt to call `setPassword("123456")` and log any errors.
5. Attempt to call `setPassword("NoUppercase123!")` and log any errors.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Password updates successfully.
- Steps 3–5: System rejects invalid passwords (e.g., throws exception or returns false).
- No partial updates (password remains unchanged for invalid attempts).

Title: Verify email update with valid and invalid formats
Preconditions: Existing user with ID 1001 and email "john.doe@example.com"
Test Steps:
1. Call `setEmail("john.doe.new@example.org")` on user with ID 1001.
2. Verify email is updated (assume system validates format).
3. Attempt to call `setEmail("invalid.email")` and log any errors.
4. Attempt to call `setEmail("john@.com")` and log any errors.
5. Attempt to call `setEmail("missing@domain")` and log any errors.
6. Attempt to call `setEmail("john.doe@example")` and log any errors.
Expected Result:
- Step 1: Email updates successfully.
- Steps 3–6: System rejects invalid emails (e.g., throws exception or returns false).
- No partial updates (email remains unchanged for invalid attempts).

Title: Verify successful product creation with valid attributes
Preconditions: Existing user with ID 1001; system allows product creation
Test Data:
- Product name: "Vintage Chair"
- Location: "Milan, Italy"
- Image: "chair_vintage.jpg" (assume file path or URL)
- Price: 199.99
- Proprietario: User with ID 1001
Test Steps:
1. Create a new `Prodotto` object using `new()`.
2. Set attributes:
   - Name: "Vintage Chair"
   - Luogo: "Milan, Italy"
   - Immagine: "chair_vintage.jpg"
   - Prezzo: 199.99
   - Proprietario: User object with ID 1001
3. Verify all attributes are stored correctly.
Expected Result:
- Product object is created successfully with all attributes set.
- No errors during object creation or attribute assignment.
- Proprietario field correctly references the user with ID 1001.

Title: Verify product creation with invalid price (negative/zero)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to create a `Prodotto` with `Prezzo = -100.00`.
2. Log any errors or exceptions.
3. Attempt to create a `Prodotto` with `Prezzo = 0.00`.
4. Log any errors or exceptions.
5. Create a `Prodotto` with `Prezzo = 50.00` and verify success.
Expected Result:
- Steps 1–2: System rejects negative/zero prices (e.g., throws exception or returns false).
- Step 5: Product is created successfully with valid price.

Title: Verify one-to-many relationship: user owns multiple products
Preconditions: Existing user with ID 1001; no existing products
Test Data:
- User: ID 1001
- Products:
  1. Name: "Antique Lamp", Price: 299.99
  2. Name: "Wooden Table", Price: 450.50
Test Steps:
1. Create `Prodotto` object 1 with proprietario = User(1001).
2. Create `Prodotto` object 2 with proprietario = User(1001).
3. Verify both products are associated with the same user (ID 1001).
4. Attempt to create a third product with `Proprietario = null` and log behavior.
Expected Result:
- Steps 1–2: Both products are successfully linked to User(1001).
- Step 4: System either rejects `null` proprietario or assigns a default user (document behavior).

Title: Verify product ownership transfer between users
Preconditions:
- User1 (ID 1001) owns Product1 ("Vintage Chair").
- User2 (ID 1002) exists but does not own Product1.
Test Steps:
1. Retrieve Product1 owned by User1.
2. Change `Proprietario` of Product1 to User2.
3. Verify Product1 is now associated with User2.
4. Verify User1 no longer "has" Product1 (if system tracks this bidirectionally).
Expected Result:
- Step 2: Ownership transfer succeeds.
- Step 3: Product1’s `Proprietario` attribute reflects User2.
- Step 4: User1’s product list excludes Product1 (if applicable).

Title: Verify system behavior with empty/missing product attributes
Test Data:
- Missing attributes:
  - Name: (empty string)
  - Price: (not set)
  - Proprietario: (not set)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to create `Prodotto` with `Name = ""` (empty string).
2. Log any errors or exceptions.
3. Attempt to create `Prodotto` with `Prezzo` unset.
4. Log any errors or exceptions.
5. Attempt to create `Prodotto` with `Proprietario = null`.
6. Log any errors or exceptions.
Expected Result:
- Steps 1–3: System rejects products with missing mandatory fields (e.g., throws exception or returns false).
- Step 5: System either rejects `null` proprietario or assigns a default user (document behavior).

Title: Verify user attribute updates with edge-case inputs
Test Data:
- Name: "A" (single character), "John Doe Smith" (long name)
- Cognome: "X" (single character), "DoeJohnsonWilliams" (long surname)
- Email: "a@a.a" (minimal valid), "john.doe+test@example.com" (with +)
Test Steps:
1. Call `setNome("A")` and verify no errors.
2. Call `setNome("John Doe Smith")` and verify no errors.
3. Call `setCognome("X")` and verify no errors.
4. Call `setCognome("DoeJohnsonWilliams")` and verify no errors.
5. Call `setEmail("a@a.a")` and verify no errors.
6. Call `setEmail("john.doe+test@example.com")` and verify no errors.
Expected Result:
- All steps succeed; system accepts edge-case inputs within defined limits.
- No truncation or silent failures for valid but minimal/maximal inputs.
